message:
fix(core): separate process-env from layered-env lookup and fix blank-var ephemeral misclassification

The ephemeral classification system was reading dotenv layers it
shouldn't, and blank inherited env vars caused production workloads
to be misclassified as ephemeral:

- New process_env_value() reads only from the process environment (plus
  test override hook), skipping user-global and project .env files.
  env_value() now delegates to it for its first layer. infra_env_value()
  deduplicates by reusing process_env_value()
- std_env_lookup in ephemeral.rs now calls process_env_value instead of
  raw std::env::var, making it testable and consistent
- New has_nonempty_env_value() treats blank env vars as absent, fixing
  false-positive ephemeral classification when RUST_TEST_THREADS="" or
  NTM_SESSION_DIR="" are set but empty
- Tests for blank-env-var handling and test-override plumbing

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
